title: Director, Business Intelligence
description: About Hone Hone is an online medical clinic at the forefront of transforming healthcare and enhancing longevity. We use cutting-edge scientific advancements to empower men and women to take control of their health and unlock their full potential. Our people are the heart of everything we do and drive our success. We approach every project through our brand values: Fight for the Customer Execute Ruthlessly Communicate Candidly, Clearly, and Kindly Collaborate Selflessly Practice Calculated Risk-Taking Maximize Joy and Gratitude Hone has been fully virtual from day one and will continue to be a remote-first employer. Our Ideal Candidate Our ideal candidate is a mission-driven, motivated multi-tasker who is invested in work that is fulfilling and impactful. They embrace change and tackle challenges with enthusiasm. They have an “all-in” disposition towards work, understanding that we are a fast-paced, high-growth organization with evolving priorities. They can excel at both independent tasks and collaborative work, leading with clear and candid communication. They exhibit humble leadership—the ability to drive initiatives forward while remaining excited about continuous learning and development opportunities. They feel strongly about being part of a team that advocates for people to live longer and better lives. The Role Hone is looking for a Director, Business Intelligence to lead our BI function and manage a team of analysts supporting Finance & Operations, Product & Engineering, and Marketing & Customer Care. In this role, you will report to the Sr. Director, Data, Analytics, and Machine Learning and serve as a key thought partner in shaping how data informs decisions across the company. You will own the BI roadmap and operating model, including self-service analytics, governance, stakeholder engagement, and delivery. This role is responsible for ensuring BI is both responsive to business needs and proactive in surfacing insights, while maintaining high standards for data quality, consistency, and usability. This is a senior leadership role that blends strategy, people management, and hands-on analytical work. Primary Responsibilities Key responsibilities for this role include (but are not limited to) the following: BI Strategy & Roadmap Define and own the Business Intelligence roadmap, aligning BI initiatives with company priorities and data strategy. Drive the evolution of BI from ad hoc reporting to a proactive, insight-driven function. Identify opportunities where BI can better inform strategic, operational, and clinical decisions. Team Leadership & Development Lead and develop a team of ~3-5 analysts across multiple business domains and experience levels. Set clear expectations for quality, timeliness, and impact. Coach analysts on analytical rigor, stakeholder management, and storytelling. Support hiring, onboarding, and long-term team growth planning. Self-Service BI & Governance Own the self-service BI strategy, enabling stakeholders to answer common questions independently. Establish and enforce BI governance, including metric definitions, data ownership, access controls, and documentation. Partner with data engineering and analytics engineering to ensure BI is built on trusted, well-modeled data. Stakeholder Partnership & Delivery Act as the primary BI partner for senior leaders across Finance, Operations, Product, Marketing, and Customer Care. Intake, prioritize, and manage BI requests using a clear, transparent process. Balance short-term stakeholder needs with longer-term strategic initiatives. Ensure BI deliverables are clear, actionable, and decision-oriented. Hands-On Analysis & Execution Remain engaged in hands-on work as needed, including building dashboards, reviewing analyses, and tackling complex or high-impact analytical problems. Ensure dashboards and reports meet high standards for accuracy, usability, and design. Lead or support deep-dive analyses that inform strategic decisions. Operational Excellence Track team throughput, quality, and stakeholder satisfaction. Identify and address bottlenecks in BI workflows, tooling, or collaboration. Improve processes to increase speed, reliability, and impact. Qualifications In order to qualify for this role, candidates must meet the following criteria: 12+ years of experience in analytics, business intelligence, data, or a related field. 4+ years of experience managing and developing analytics or BI teams. Proven track record of owning BI strategy and delivering high-impact analytics at scale. Strong technical foundation in SQL and BI tools; ability to review and produce high-quality dashboards and analyses. Experience designing self-service BI solutions and metric governance frameworks. Strong project and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to juggle competing priorities. Experience partnering closely with senior leaders across multiple business functions. Excellent communication and data storytelling skills. Experience working with modern data stacks (e.g., dbt, cloud data warehouses, modern BI tools). Familiarity with product analytics, financial reporting, and operational metrics. Experience working closely with data engineering and machine learning teams. Exposure to experimentation or causal analysis frameworks. Experience in healthcare, telehealth, or other regulated industries. Benefits* Hone wants our team to be in the best condition of their lives, so we offer a range of benefits including: A remote-first work environment Competitive compensation and equity options Health, dental, and vision insurance coverage Short-term disability and basic life coverage Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs) Lifestyle Spending Accounts (LSAs) We follow federal holidays and have uncapped time off Budget for the technology tools you need (laptop, monitor, and/or special software) A focus on company-sponsored activities to foster engagement (both virtual and in-person) Waived membership fees for any Hone team members utilizing Hone products *These benefits are available to full-time, regular employees, and not to independent contractors, hourly or temporary employees, or interns. We are proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace committed to building a team culture that celebrates diversity and inclusion. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions. Please contact us to request accommodation.
